# Security Policy for TranscriptG

We take the security of **TranscriptG** seriously. If you believe you have found a security vulnerability, we appreciate your help in reporting it to us privately so we can patch it as quickly as possible.

---

## Supported Versions

Only the latest release of TranscriptG is actively supported with security updates. We recommend always keeping your local forks up to date with the main repository.

| Version | Supported |
| ------- | --------- |
| Latest  | ✅ Yes    |
| Older   | ❌ No     |

---

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Please do not report security vulnerabilities via public GitHub issues.**

Instead, please report security issues directly to our administrator:

* **Email**: [akashsinghsolanki66@gmail.com](mailto:akashsinghsolanki66@gmail.com)
* **Official Website**: [TranscriptG Support](https://transcriptg.com/)

### What to Include in Your Report
To help us triage and resolve the issue quickly, please include:
1. A detailed description of the vulnerability.
2. Step-by-step instructions (or a proof-of-concept script/exploit) to reproduce the behavior.
3. The potential impact of the vulnerability if exploited.

### Our Response Commitment
We will acknowledge receipt of your vulnerability report within **48 hours** and work diligently to provide a resolution or patch in a timely manner. We appreciate your cooperation in practicing responsible disclosure.
